results.title: Accelerating Deployment of Additional EV Chargers at HDB Car Parks
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that EV chargers are installed in all HDB towns and are being scaled up alongside EV adoption, prioritizing carparks with high charging demand. This expansion is subject to available electrical capacity. The Minister did not specify a timeline for accelerating deployment or provide reasons for any delays beyond the stated constraint of electrical capacity.

results.title: Addressing Unfair Booking Practices of Practical Driving Lessons Using Bots and Additional Measures to Address Issues
results.summary: To address high demand and unfair bot usage for Class 3 practical driving lessons, driving schools are hiring more instructors, including foreigners, to reduce wait times. Anti-bot measures such as CAPTCHA, one-time password verification, and booking restrictions have been implemented, with accounts suspended for violators. The Traffic Police confirmed no plans to reissue private driving instructor licences, maintaining the 1987 policy to ensure professional, standardised driver education. These actions aim to improve access and fairness without altering the licensing framework.

results.title: Adding Congenital Conditions into List for Chronic Disease Management Programme
results.summary: The Ministry of Health declined to list additional congenital conditions like hypogonadism under the Chronic Disease Management Programme (CDMP), citing that such conditions are specialist-led, involve complex therapies, are not routinely managed in primary care, and are less prevalent in the general population. The Clinical Advisory Committee reviews CDMP inclusions based on disease burden, intervention benefits, cumulative costs, and evidence-based guidelines. While CHAS subsidies are available at polyclinics and private GP clinics for chronic disease management, MediFund is restricted to polyclinics and public hospitals due to stricter financial governance oversight. Needy CHAS patients requiring additional financial assistance can access MediFund at polyclinics for their chronic conditions, whereas MediFund is not extended to private GP clinics.

results.title: Additional Subsidies or Financial Support for Senior Households Living in HDB Flats on Fixed or No Incomes
results.summary: The Government supports seniors aging in HDB flats through the Lease Buyback Scheme, offering up to $30,000 cash bonuses alongside proceeds from partial lease sales. Living cost relief includes permanent GST Vouchers comprising cash, MediSave, U-Save, and Service/Conservancy Charge rebates, plus quarterly Silver Support payments enhanced in 2025 to up to $1,080 for eligible low-income retirees. Additional measures cover property tax rebates, Assurance Package payments, and subsidised in-home fittings via the Enhancement for Active Seniors programme. While the Lease Buyback Scheme uptake remains low at 12,700 households as of June 2024, the Ministry acknowledges high cost-of-living concerns and will closely review further support options to encourage monetisation and reduce financial burdens.

results.title: Adequacy of ATMs for Withdrawing and Depositing Cash within Existing and New HDB Estates
results.summary: Currently, over 1,600 off-premise ATMs and more than 150 retail branches operate across Singapore, with over 1,200 ATMs located within HDB towns. Both ATM and branch counts have declined by an average of 2% annually over the past decade as banks rationalise networks due to rising adoption of online banking and cashless payments. The Monetary Authority of Singapore monitors ATM numbers and engages banks to ensure adequate access, considering factors like population density and footfall when siting facilities. Banks prioritise central locations such as heartland malls and food centres, while also partnering with retail outlets like 7-Eleven and Giant to enable cash withdrawals at purchase points in both mature and non-mature estates. MAS will continue collaborating with banks to maintain sufficient banking facilities while encouraging digital banking usage.

results.title: Adjusting Peak Hours for Bus Services in Light of Increased Flexible and Hybrid Work Arrangements
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority and bus operators adjust service frequencies based on actual ridership and evolving commuter travel patterns rather than fixed peak hours. Operators increase service frequency during off-peak periods only when sufficient demand exists. The Ministry has not specified a new timeline for adjusting stipulated peak hours or mandated increased frequency beyond traditional periods. Current operations remain responsive to real-time demand data instead of rigid scheduling changes.

results.title: Affordability of Utility Bills for Singaporeans Given Rising Energy Costs and Push Towards Green Energy Alternatives
results.summary: The Government balances sustainability, energy security, and affordability by linking electricity prices to imported fuel costs while offering support measures. Eligible households can use Climate Vouchers under the enhanced Climate Friendly Household Programme to offset costs of switching to energy-efficient appliances. Additionally, most HDB households receive U-Save rebates, with eligible households set to receive up to $760 in 2025, complemented by Community Development Council Vouchers. Households are encouraged to adopt energy-saving habits like setting air-conditioning to 25°C and turning off unused appliances. Businesses can also invest in energy efficiency supported by Government grants.

results.title: Allowing Season Parking for Personal Mobility Devices in HDB Multi-Storey Car Parks
results.summary: The Ministry of National Development addressed the query regarding season parking for personal mobility devices in HDB multi-storey car parks. This matter was previously answered in an official reply issued on 12 January 2026. The Minister for National Development, Mr Chee Hong Tat, confirmed that the specific question was already handled in that prior response. No new measures, timelines, or policy changes were announced during this session.

results.title: Appeal Avenues for Bankrupt against Decisions Managed by Official Assignee Under Private Trustees in Bankruptcy Regime
results.summary: Under the Private Trustees in Bankruptcy regime, a bankrupt whose estate is administered by a private trustee may apply to the High Court to review any act, omission, or decision of that trustee. Alternatively, the bankrupt may lodge a complaint with the Official Assignee regarding the private trustee's conduct in administering the estate. The Official Assignee operates independently from the Ministry of Law's Insolvency Office and does not manage private trustee decisions. These are the specific avenues available for challenging private trustee actions, distinct from the process involving the Official Assignee as the trustee.

results.title: Appropriateness of New Zealand's Smoke-free Generation Law for Singapore
results.summary: The Ministry for Health clarified that New Zealand has withdrawn its smoke-free generation law, while the UK announced plans in October 2023 to ban tobacco purchases for those born in 2009 or later. Singapore remains open to studying a similar cohort ban but emphasizes its existing robust strategy, which includes tax prohibitions and cessation programmes, achieving an all-time low smoking prevalence of 8.4% in 2024. The Minister noted concerns regarding implementation challenges observed in other jurisdictions and stated that Singapore will continue monitoring international developments. Specific estimates on revenue impact, healthcare cost savings, or public polling findings for Singapore were not provided, as the focus remains on solving current smoking concerns through existing measures.

results.title: Asbestos Testing and Investigations of Coloured Play Sand Products with Safety Measures Following International Recalls
results.summary: Following international recalls of asbestos-containing coloured play sand, the Consumer Product Safety Office (CPSO) and National Environment Agency (NEA) confirmed no affected products are sold by major local retailers. NEA has removed listings from two e-commerce platforms and conducted selective market testing, detecting no asbestos content. Consequently, the agencies determined there are no significant safety risks locally, eliminating the need for interim safety guidelines for preschools and indoor playgrounds. The Government will continue monitoring the situation and engaging stakeholders should potential product safety concerns arise.

results.title: Assessment Framework for Selection of Water Treatment Technology Vis-à-vis Long-term Resource Efficiency and Sustainability
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment clarified that while NEWater plants recycle used water, desalination plants are necessary to diversify supply because NEWater production is limited by available used water volume. The Government plans to mitigate the higher energy intensity and carbon footprint of desalination by continuing research and development on energy-efficient technologies for both processes. Sustainability and long-term resource efficiency are weighed through this ongoing technological advancement rather than by restricting desalination capacity.

results.title: Assistance for Those Living In One- to Two-Persons Households At Risk of Social Isolation Or Who Need Post-hospitalisation Home Care
results.summary: The Government addresses social isolation risks for seniors in one- to two-person households through the Age Well SG initiative, which evolved Active Ageing Centres to engage vulnerable individuals. The Ministry of Health established the Agency for Integrated Care and recruited Silver Generation Ambassadors to expand outreach networks. Enhanced support includes Home Personal Care services for post-hospitalisation needs. The Minister confirmed continued strengthening of efforts via partnerships with volunteer groups and community organisations to befriend and support isolated seniors, though specific expansion timelines or new scheme names were not detailed in the response.

results.title: Benefits from Strategic Food Partnership with New Zealand for Singaporeans and Local Businesses
results.summary: Launched in October 2025, the Strategic Food Partnership (SFP) with New Zealand aims to strengthen agri-food trade, supply chains, biosecurity, and research. Under the accompanying Agreement on Trade in Essential Supplies, both nations can continue receiving food items during disruptions. Initiatives include expanding regionalisation for Highly Pathogenic Avian Influenza to cover African Swine Fever and Foot and Mouth Disease, thereby minimising trade interruptions. The partnership also explores facilitating stakeholder exchanges between public and private sectors to identify pain points and commercialise investment opportunities. While the Minister noted potential areas for regulatory framework improvements, research cooperation, and long-term offtake agreements, she deferred detailed elaboration on global partnership strategies and specific collaboration types to a separate Parliamentary Question or the Committee of Supply debate regarding Singapore Food Story 2.

results.title: Breakdown of Existing COE Holder Bidding Patterns Based on Certificate Expiry Timeline
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport provided a breakdown of new COE registrations awarded to existing holders in Q1–Q3 2025 for Categories A and B. The data categorizes bids by the remaining validity of the previous COE at the time of bidding, specifically within one year, one to three years, three to five years, and over five years. Detailed absolute numbers and percentages for each validity period are presented in Table 1. The specific figures contained within the table are not included in this summary.

results.title: Building Sustainability Reporting Capabilities for SGX-listed Small and Mid-size Companies
results.summary: To support small and mid-sized SGX-listed companies during the delayed climate reporting implementation, Enterprise Singapore and the Economic Development Board extended the Sustainability Reporting Grant (SRG) and provided access to the SME Sustainability Hub. The Accounting and Corporate Regulatory Authority issued a Sustainability Reporting Body of Knowledge to guide training providers in developing relevant courses. These measures aim to help listed companies build sustainability capabilities aligned with International Sustainability Standards while the Government reviews support mechanisms for a low-carbon future.

results.title: Building on Working Relationship with Saudi Authorities to Manage Haj Arrangements and Quotas for Singaporeans
results.summary: Singapore maintains a strong working relationship with Saudi Arabia's Ministry of Hajj and Umrah, currently holding a quota of 900 places for 2026. The Islamic Religious Council of Singapore (MUIS) administers these allocations via the MyHajSG portal on a first-come, first-served basis, with priority given to senior pilgrims aged 70 and above. Starting in 2026, all pilgrims must present a valid medical health certificate, for which MUIS and the Ministry of Health are coordinating with local providers. While the standard quota formula is 0.1% of the country's Muslim population, Singapore has proactively requested additional places, and leaders will continue to engage Saudi counterparts to improve future allocation chances.

results.title: Capacity and Utilisation Rates of Current Childcare Places in Bidadari Estate and Projected Demand for Next Three Years
results.summary: As of 2025, the Toa Payoh Planning Area, including Bidadari, holds approximately 860 infant and 8,700 childcare places with utilisation rates of 66% and 73% respectively, both below national averages. To address projected demand, the Early Childhood Development Agency is deploying new preschools adding 125 infant and 640 childcare places in the coming months, primarily in Bidadari. These new facilities will be operated mostly by Anchor Operators. Current supply remains underutilised relative to national benchmarks, and the expansion aims to align capacity with future housing developments.

results.title: Clarification by Leader of the House
results.summary: Leader of the House corrected a previous statement regarding the Legal Profession Act, clarifying that Section 94A (not Section 94) governs referrals for legal practitioners convicted of fraud or dishonesty. This referral process is handled by the Law Society, AGC, and Courts, not Parliament. The correction updates the record from a January 14, 2026 sitting, confirming that eligibility for referral depends on specific convictions under the amended section. No new timeline or operational constraint was introduced beyond this statutory clarification.

results.title: Clarification by Minister of State for National Development
results.summary: Minister of State for National Development Alvin Tan issued a factual correction to his 14 January 2026 reply regarding the Animal and Bird Act (ABA). He clarified that breaches of the relevant code support prosecution under the ABA. Specifically, unnecessary pain or suffering constitutes an offence under section 42, while failure of duty of care is an offence under section 41C. Upon finding such breaches, NParks may take appropriate enforcement action. The Minister confirmed these sections apply to enable prosecution and enforcement.

results.title: Cooperation between SNOC and NSAs to Achieve Sporting Excellence
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth outlined how SportSG and the Singapore National Olympic Council (SNOC) collaborate to enhance National Sport Associations' (NSAs) capabilities beyond administrative and funding support. Since 2021, SNOC affiliation criteria mandate NSAs to adopt SportSG-recommended governance principles, with agencies seconding personnel to selected NSAs for governance and administration support. Athlete development is facilitated through the High Performance Sport Institute's expertise, Major Games preparation programmes, and the SNOC Athletes' Commission, which encourages appointing athlete representatives for policy creation. Coaching excellence is advanced via CoachSG's SG-Coach programmes, the National Registry of Coaches, and IOC-funded Olympic Solidarity Programmes for training. Community engagement is strengthened through the Singapore Olympic Academy's school programmes and the annual Singapore Sports Awards to recognise outstanding individuals.

results.title: Cross-Border Industrial Pollution Coordination and Transboundary Environmental and Air Quality Monitoring Standards
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment directed Members' queries regarding cross-border industrial pollution coordination, joint sampling exercises, and ambient air quality standards for volatile organic compounds to a combined written reply. This response was issued in Parliamentary Question No 69 at the 13 January 2026 Parliament sitting. The summary does not include specific details on sampling frequencies, analytical methods, detection limits, or comparative standards against WHO, EU, or US guidelines as these were not explicitly stated in the transcript. Members are advised to refer to the full written reply for comprehensive data on monitoring protocols and regulatory comparisons.

results.title: Data Validation of Mean Kilometres Between Failure Reports and Verification Processes to Address Potential Reporting Oversight
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority (LTA) independently determines whether incidents meet the five-minute threshold for Mean Kilometres Between Failure (MKBF) calculations. LTA identifies qualifying delays by automatically reviewing full train movement data logged by each line's control system, rather than relying solely on operator reports. This process ensures that only service delays exceeding five minutes are included in the metric, with LTA conducting the necessary verification without external dependency on reported durations.

results.title: Data of Median Net Monthly Income of Residents Engaged as Direct Selling Distributors
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) does not track the median net monthly income of residents engaged as direct selling distributors or those using multi-level compensation structures. This is because MOM's labour market income statistics adhere to standard international industry classifications rather than specific business models. For income data on own account workers, the public is directed to refer to statistical tabulations published on MOM's website. The Ministry has not indicated plans to require entities to report earnings data to ensure income transparency.

results.title: Data on Appeals Received for Secondary 1 Posting Exercise and Top Reasons Given for Appealing
results.summary: Following the 2025 Secondary 1 Posting Exercise, the Ministry of Education received approximately 150 appeals for school changes, though data on direct appeals to schools is unavailable as reporting is not mandatory. Most appeals request a school closer to home or one with specific co-curricular offerings. The Minister noted that a small percentage of parents and students typically appeal annually, with some writing directly to MOE while others approach schools directly.

results.title: Data on Caning Sentences Including Age and Gender Exemptions from 2015 to 2025
results.summary: In 2024, 279 individuals were convicted of serious sexual or violent offences attracting caning. Of these, 76 males under 50 received at least one stroke, 53 males aged 50 or above were exempted, and 13 were females. The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs noted that comprehensive data spanning 2015 to 2025 is not readily available. The breakdown covers specific severe offences including rape, sexual assault, and culpable homicide.

results.title: Data on Compliance Inspection of Buildings with PWD Holding Points and Measures to Enforce Retrofitting of PWD Holding Points
results.summary: Under the SCDF Fire Code 2023, building owners must appoint a Registered Inspector to certify compliance with PWD holding point requirements before occupation. SCDF conducts routine enforcement checks to ensure escape routes and access to these points remain unobstructed. Based on checks from 2021 to 2025, no non-compliance related to PWD holding points was detected. The SCDF does not track the retrofitting status of buildings constructed prior to the current PWD holding point requirements in the Fire Code.

results.title: Data on Efficacy of Masks and Spit Guards Leading to Removal from Food Handlers' Requirement from 1 January 2026
results.summary: The Singapore Food Agency removed the mandatory mask and spit guard requirement for food handlers effective 1 January 2026, citing analysis that shows these items pose low food safety risk compared to key contamination drivers like inadequate sanitation and improper temperature management. While not legally required, the agency encourages proper usage to prevent cross-contamination and advises handlers with respiratory symptoms to refrain from handling food. The removal aligns with the agency's ongoing review of public hygiene measures to ensure relevance to current food safety risks.

results.title: Data on Food Wastage Generated by Meals Rejected from Senior Citizen Food Delivery Programmes and Efforts to Address Wastage
results.summary: The Ministry of Health does not track daily food waste data for senior citizen meal delivery services. Instead, government-funded providers address wastage by aligning delivery schedules with client availability and tailoring meals to specific dietary needs. No average rejection statistics or specific waste-reduction actions beyond these operational adjustments have been reported since 2022.

results.title: Data on HDB Blocks That Did Not Garner 75% Votes for Home Improvement Programmes
results.summary: In the last 10 years, six HDB blocks failed to secure the required 75% vote from eligible Singapore Citizen households for the Home Improvement Programme (HIP), including recent unsuccessful polls in Tiong Bahru in November 2025. Following appeals, HDB allowed four blocks at Serangoon and Lengkong Tiga to re-poll approximately one year later, all of which subsequently succeeded. The Minister noted that smaller blocks face higher risks of failing the threshold and is reviewing the voting process, potentially by grouping low-unit blocks into clusters to ensure sufficient participation. While HIP addresses wear and tear, HDB also offers the Goodwill Repair Assistance scheme for ceiling leaks and spalling concrete, as well as the Enhancement for Active Seniors programme for senior-friendly fittings, with HDB co-paying costs for eligible households.

results.title: Data on Local Jobseekers without Updates from Potential Employers Beyond 45 Days After Interviews
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower does not collect data on the proportion of local jobseekers who receive no employer updates beyond 45 days post-interview. While the Tripartite Alliance for Fair and Progressive Employment Practices has published a Fair Recruitment and Selection Handbook encouraging employers to communicate results within a reasonable timeframe, no specific new initiatives to enforce timely feedback were announced. The Ministry continues to encourage constructive feedback exchange where practical and will monitor support mechanisms for workers in the job search process.

results.title: Data on Reported Cases of Challenges Faced by Non-guardian Caretakers in Accessing Child-related Services or Assistance
results.summary: The Minister for Social and Family Development clarified that appointed caregivers, including foster parents and kin carers, hold decision authority for day-to-day care and can access financial subsidies for school and healthcare under Care and Protection Orders. For substantive matters like overseas travel, education, and medical treatment, decision authority remains with birth parents due to long-term implications. If birth parents cannot be contacted or are unable/unwilling to decide after reasonable efforts, appointed caregivers may decide in consultation with MSF, fostering agencies, qualified persons, or the Director-General of Social Welfare. Specific case numbers regarding reported difficulties were not provided in the response.

results.title: Demographics of Executive Condominium Buyers and Breakdown of Loan-to-Value Distribution for Past Five Years
results.summary: From 2021 to 2025, approximately 40% of Executive Condominium (EC) purchasers were first-timers. The Housing and Development Board does not track loan-to-value ratios for EC purchases because these properties are financed by housing loans from financial institutions rather than government schemes. Consequently, specific data on buyer loan-to-value distribution percentages for the past five years is unavailable.

results.title: Deployment of AI, Augmented Reality or Extended Reality-based Multilingual Narration at Tourist Spots while Safeguarding Tourist Guides Position
results.summary: The Singapore Tourism Board (STB) is piloting augmented reality (AR) and artificial intelligence (AI) solutions, such as "Merli's Immersive Adventure" and chatbots, to enhance visitor experiences at iconic spots. These technologies are intended to supplement, not substitute, licensed tourist guides, with STB providing grants via the Experience Step-Up Fund to support business adoption. To ensure the licensing framework remains meaningful, STB is upskilling guides through live workshops on leveraging AI and digitalisation. An updated licence renewal framework, "Learn + Do," will take effect from 1 March 2026, encouraging guides to take ownership of their learning in technology adoption to deliver unique experiences.

results.title: Designating Areas for Busking without a Licence
results.summary: Under the Public Entertainments Act, busking in Singapore requires a Letter of Endorsement (LOE) from the National Arts Council (NAC) rather than a standard license, as mandated by the Public Entertainments (Exemptions) Order. The NAC collaborates with venue owners and authorities to designate specific locations, such as in Chinatown, suitable for busking activities to enhance vibrancy while preserving public order and preventing local disamenities. Community groups interested in performing at these NAC-designated sites may apply for an LOE through auditions. The NAC continues to review the list of designated busking locations to manage space usage effectively in land-scarce Singapore.

results.title: Discouraging Teenagers from Cycling in Groups without Wearing Helmets
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that cyclists must wear helmets and adhere to group size and formation requirements on roads. The Land Transport Authority and Traffic Police regularly conduct school talks to promote safe cycling practices. Additionally, the Active Mobility Community Ambassadors scheme engages volunteers to encourage path sharing, while joint enforcement operations with the Traffic Police are conducted to deter offenders. No specific new measures or timelines for discouraging group cycling without helmets were announced beyond existing regulations and enforcement activities.

results.title: Encouraging Solar Panel Installation in Private and Landed Properties
results.summary: The Government will simplify regulations to encourage solar adoption in private developments and landed properties, rather than imposing mandatory requirements. Key facilitation measures include the Energy Market Authority's Simplified Credit Treatment Scheme and Enhanced Central Intermediary Scheme, which allow owners to register installations with SP Group for direct bill payments. With declining panel costs, residential systems may now have a payback period as short as five years. As of June 2025, over 1.7 GWp of capacity has been installed, progressing toward the 2030 target of 2 GWp.

results.title: Enforcement Applications Filed in Respect of Community Disputes Resolution Tribunal Orders Issued between 2020 and 2024
results.summary: Between 2020 and 2024, 69 Special Direction applications and 13 Exclusion Order applications were filed regarding Community Disputes Resolution Tribunal (CDRT) orders, resulting in 24 Special Directions and two Exclusion Orders. This yields an approximate 30% enforcement success rate, though the Minister cautioned that small sample sizes subject these percentages to wide statistical fluctuations. Breaching a CDRT order constitutes contempt of court, punishable by fines up to $5,000 or imprisonment for up to three months. While private claimants typically monitor compliance, the Community Relations Unit in the pilot town of Tampines can assist by using investigation powers to verify breaches of CDRT orders or Special Directions in severe noise nuisance and hoarding cases.

results.title: Extending Wireless Alert Alarm System to Vulnerable Seniors Living Alone in Owner-occupied HDB Flats
results.summary: The Housing and Development Board is currently prioritizing the rollout of the wireless Alert Alarm System (wAAS) for seniors in public rental housing, noting that expanding to owner-occupied flats requires additional resources for round-the-clock response operators. For residents in sold flats, HDB partners with commercial vendors to offer fall detection and response packages that automatically alert next-of-kin or care providers upon a fall, potentially activating an ambulance. Additionally, eligible seniors in owner-occupied flats can access 24/7 technology-enabled monitoring and response for fall detection under the Ministry of Health's Enhanced Home Personal Care service, which will be rolled out islandwide later this year.

results.title: Feasibility of Opting Out of Using Retirement Account Top-up Contributions to Pay CPF LIFE Premiums
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower stated that top-ups to the Retirement Account (RA) must be committed to long-term retirement needs, as they earn a higher long-term interest rate. Consequently, CPF LIFE members topping up their RA will have their full top-up amounts streamed out as higher monthly payouts via CPF LIFE throughout retirement. The Board will not consider allowing members to opt out of using top-up contributions to pay CPF LIFE premiums, even if the member has a sufficient RA balance for a minimum annuity payout.

results.title: Funding for Teachers to Purchase Work-related Mobile Phones for Parent-teacher Communications
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that schools possess official channels, including the Parents Gateway app, school email, and designated phone lines, which adequately support parent-teacher communication. Consequently, teachers are not expected to communicate with parents outside school hours, and there is no need for funding to purchase work-related mobile phones or separate lines. For urgent contact during off-premises activities, schools provide school-owned devices or facilitate communication through the general office. Therefore, no funding is available for teachers to acquire personal work-related mobile phones for these purposes.

results.title: HDB Design Improvements for Enhanced Privacy Amid Declining Satisfaction Levels on Noise and Cleanliness
results.summary: The Minister stated that the 2023/2024 Sample Household Survey, involving over 7,000 households, found more than nine in 10 residents satisfied with flat sizes, privacy, and facilities. To enhance privacy, HDB ensures adequate spacing between blocks, designs windows to face external landscapes rather than corridors, and incorporates frosted glass panels for toilets and visual screens along common corridors. Regarding noise, blocks near major roads and mass rapid transit tracks are orientated away from noise sources where possible. For cleanliness, Town Councils manage estate maintenance while the Ministry of National Development publishes an annual performance report. The Minister confirmed HDB will continue offering various flat types from 2-room Flexi to 3-Generation flats to meet diverse resident needs.

results.title: Hit-and-run Cases where Offenders were Cyclists or AMD Users
results.summary: Between 2023 and November 2025, there were 12 reported hit-and-run accidents involving cyclists or active mobility device (AMD) users, with eight resulting in pedestrian injuries. Only one suspect has been identified to date. Investigations face specific challenges due to the lack of registration numbers and limited camera coverage at accident locations. The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs noted these complexities hinder police investigation leads.

results.title: Immigration Status Granted for Individuals Aged 65 and above Since June 2024
results.summary: From June to December 2024, the Ministry of Home Affairs granted Singapore Citizenship, Permanent Residency, Employment Passes, S Passes, and Dependant's Passes to individuals aged 65 and above. Specific counts for this period are provided in an accompanying table, while 2025 figures remain unavailable. The Minister confirmed these statistics reflect the total approvals issued during the stated timeframe. No further breakdown or policy rationale was provided in the transcript.

results.title: Impact of AI Translation Tools on Tour Guide Services in Singapore
results.summary: In June 2024, the Singapore Tourism Board (STB) removed the Multi-Language Proficiency Test requirement, allowing tourist guides to self-declare language proficiency without formal testing. This regulatory change aims to streamline licensing while maintaining accountability to hirers and tourists for demonstrating competencies. STB reports no adverse complaints from visitors and notes that travel agents appreciate the flexibility to secure multilingual guides for market demand. Although AI translation tools enable independent destination discovery, the Board emphasizes that human guides provide unique experiences beyond mere translation. STB remains committed to advancing the profession to help guides leverage AI technologies to enhance tourist experiences.

results.title: Impact of Recently-announced Plans to Upgrade of SMRT Trains on Train Capacity and Service Frequency
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport clarified that the planned mid-life upgrade of 92 SMRT trains on the North-South and East-West Lines aims to improve passenger experience, energy efficiency, and reliability, rather than extending service life. Upgrades involve enhancing air-conditioning, ventilation, and condition-monitoring sensors. The Ministry confirmed there are sufficient trains in the fleet to implement these upgrades without impacting service capacity or frequency. The upgrade is not intended to increase the service lifespan of the affected trains.

results.title: Impact of Social Media Platforms' Algorithmic Content Recommendation Systems on Distribution of Scam Content to Users
results.summary: Under the Online Criminal Harms Act, the Police enforce ex ante anti-scam requirements on platforms, including advertiser verification and user reporting, rather than mandating algorithmic changes. The Government considers direct assessment of content distribution algorithms difficult and intends to tighten existing anti-scam requirements, with details to be announced soon. The Minister stated that requiring platforms to display only explicitly followed or searched content is unreasonable, as it could hinder legitimate advertising and fail to stop scam ads featuring requested content. Platforms must adjust their systems as necessary to comply with these requirements.

results.title: Implementing CPF Lifetime Retirement Investment Scheme to Enable CPF Members an Option for Higher Expected Returns
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower is in the final stages of finalizing the CPF Lifetime Retirement Investment Scheme, a measure originally accepted in 2016 to offer members an option for higher expected returns through a balanced risk-return portfolio. While specific product details and timelines remain under review, the Ministry confirms that publicity initiatives to enhance financial literacy will be rolled out concurrently with the scheme's launch. The construct will incorporate glide paths and weightages on global equities versus bonds, with the primary objective of safeguarding retirement adequacy despite market turbulence. Members can currently access similar investment opportunities through existing low-cost funds under the CPF Investment Scheme while awaiting the official announcement.

results.title: Inclusion of Verification of PWD Holding Points and Accessibility Checks in Fire Certificate Inspections and Audits, and Complaints Received on Non-functional PWD Holding Points in Past Five Years
results.summary: Under the Singapore Civil Defence Force's Fire Certificate Scheme, building owners must appoint external parties for annual inspections that verify communication devices and visual alarm systems at Persons with Disabilities (PWD) holding points. The SCDF audits these inspections and conducts routine enforcement checks to ensure escape routes and access to PWD holding points remain unobstructed. The Minister stated that no complaints regarding non-functional PWD holding points were received between 2021 and 2025.

results.title: Increasing Healthy Food Options at Hawker Centres and Food Courts
results.summary: The Health Promotion Board's Healthier Dining Programme, active as of September 2025, involves over 9,200 participating stalls offering at least one lower-calorie or healthier-ingredient meal. The Healthier Ingredient Development Scheme supports operators by enabling access to healthier ingredients like lower-sodium salt at prices comparable to regular products. The Minister noted that consumers are encouraged to directly request healthier options to create demand and encourage menu adjustments. No new financial incentives were announced in this session.

results.title: Independent Testing to Verify Cybersecurity Capabilities of Electric Public Bus Manufacturer and Plans to Share Findings
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority (LTA) has conducted technical reviews with Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs), receiving assurance that existing electric buses lack remote-control capabilities. LTA is currently performing additional independent technical assessments to further verify these claims. The Government has stated it will not publicly share the findings of such cybersecurity assessments due to security implications. Consequently, there is no commitment to publish the results of these independent tests.

results.title: Informing Foreign Vehicle Drivers of Singapore's Traffic, Parking, Safety and Emission Rules Prior to Entry
results.summary: Foreign motorists entering Singapore must familiarize themselves with local laws and obtain necessary entry permits. Vehicles with multiple outstanding fines are denied entry until composition sums or Court fines are paid. The Acting Minister for Transport clarified that drivers are responsible for knowing regulations prior to arrival. No specific plans to improve post-incident resolution processes or strengthen deterrence measures like higher penalties were detailed in this response.

results.title: Making A Declaration when ICA Applications for Visas, Permanent Residence or Citizenship Are Prepared by Third Party
results.summary: The Minister for Home Affairs stated that the Immigration and Checkpoints Authority (ICA) does not endorse commercial entities or consultants claiming to improve success rates for Permanent Residence or Singapore Citizenship applications. Applicants engaging third parties are advised to exercise caution, as the onus remains solely on the applicant to ensure submitted information is true, accurate, and complete. ICA considers the involvement of a third party irrelevant to application validity because legal responsibility for any false declarations rests entirely with the applicant. Consequently, no new declaration requirement is proposed, as deviating from this principle could enable spurious defences against false statements.

results.title: Measures to Prevent Foreign Taxis from Offering Point-to-point Trips and Allowing Singapore Taxis to Access Subsidised Fuel with New Cross-border Taxi Agreement
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport directed that the response to the query regarding enforcement measures against foreign taxis, fair competition safeguards, and negotiations for subsidised fuel access for Singaporean taxis in Malaysia be provided via a written reply. This written response was issued for Question No 22 in the 13 January 2026 Parliament Sitting. No further details on specific enforcement actions, cost mitigation strategies, or the status of fuel subsidy negotiations were stated in the transcript.

results.title: Mitigation Measures for Private Preschools Serving Children with Special Needs and Support Frameworks to Encourage Viability of Such Services
results.summary: The Minister stated that ECDA maintains quality standards for Anchor and Partner Operator schemes through rigorous selection based on track record, financial sustainability, and accessibility. To ensure access for children with developmental needs, ECDA is expanding Development Support and Learning Support programmes to cover 80% of five- to six-year-olds at steady state, regardless of centre type. Inclusion Coordinators are appointed in all preschools to identify needs and refer parents to resources, while the newly published Inclusive Preschool Practice Guide offers best practices for operators. ECDA funds providers of the Development Support and Learning Support programmes and the Early Intervention Programme for Infants and Children to ensure specialised services are available. The response did not specify the exact assessment methodology for mitigating crowding out of private preschools serving special needs children or detail targeted funding options beyond existing programme funding.

results.title: Monitoring and Enforcement of Noise Threshold Compliance of Construction Noise near Residential Estates
results.summary: The National Environment Agency (NEA) enforces construction noise thresholds for sites within 150 metres of residences, requiring noise meter installation and monitoring. Enforcement actions escalate for repeated offences or prolonged exposure above permissible levels, with penalties including fines up to $40,000 for convictions and daily fines of up to $1,000 for continuing offences. While current regulations rely on five-minute averaging, the Ministry is reviewing the framework to better address disruptive short bursts and is studying the use of Lmax alongside World Health Organization guidelines. Psycho-acoustic factors like sharpness and roughness are under review but not yet incorporated into the existing noise threshold calculations.

results.title: NEA Response Protocol for Persistent Mosquito Complaints Despite Clean Inspections
results.summary: When residents report mosquito nuisance despite clean NEA inspections, the agency conducts targeted inspections to identify breeding habitats and collaborates with Town Councils and premises operators to enhance prevention measures. NEA cross-references resident feedback with surveillance data from Gravitraps to pinpoint areas with higher mosquito populations and potential breeding sources. This protocol ensures that persistent complaints are addressed through data-driven surveillance and stakeholder engagement rather than relying solely on visual inspection results.

results.title: Negotiations with Malaysian Agencies on Cross-border Industrial Pollution from Johor
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment stated that queries regarding negotiations with Malaysian agencies on cross-border industrial pollution from Johor have been addressed in a combined written reply to Parliamentary Question No 69 at the 13 January 2026 Parliament Sitting. The transcript does not specify the current status of these negotiations, the inclusion of real-time hazardous air pollutant data sharing, or mutual assistance protocols for industrial emergencies. No further details on the outcome or specific measures were provided during this session.

results.title: Other Uses of HDB Multi-storey Car Parks Aside from Rooftop Gardening
results.summary: The Housing and Development Board (HDB) has converted upper levels of multi-storey car parks (MSCPs) into greenery and community gardens where suitable. Additionally, HDB repurposed spaces at several MSCPs into courier hubs under the Courier Hub Scheme for parcel sorting and handling. Future requests to repurpose MSCPs for other community-benefiting uses will be considered subject to site-specific assessments regarding technical feasibility and safety. The Ministry has not identified other flexible uses beyond these specific conversions and pending community benefit proposals.

results.title: Parking of Personal Mobility Devices and Personal Mobility Aids at HDB Multi-storey Car Parks for Safety
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the Ministry's position on parking personal mobility devices (PMD) and personal mobility aids (PMA) in HDB multi-storey car parks was previously addressed in a reply to Mr Kenneth Tiong on 12 January 2026. No new measures regarding fire-resistant charging infrastructure or alternative parking locations were announced during this session. The existing policy framework remains unchanged, and no further action or timeline for implementation was provided.

results.title: Percentage of Critical Services Boards Comprising Members with Professional Experience in ICT Sector
results.summary: The Government does not track the percentage of board members with ICT sector experience for critical services operators. Responsibility for determining the appropriate mix of skills, knowledge, and experience lies with each individual board. Boards must assess their specific operational context to decide if ICT expertise is required, a process identical to evaluating other expertise areas like legal or finance. No specific measures or timelines for improving ICT representation were stated.

results.title: Periodic Relocation of Popular Schools to Better Align School Access with Principles of Meritocracy and Social Mixing
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) prioritizes meeting national and localised demand for school places, relocating schools—including popular ones—to areas with higher demand when population projections decline. MOE views social mixing as vital for holistic development and 21st-century competencies, implementing it through combined co-curricular activities (CCAs), inter-school teams like the BMW girls' football squad, boarding programmes, and the Singapore Youth Festival. While admissions phases balance practicality, long-standing ties, and proximity, MOE does not currently use housing type data to prioritise social mixing measures for specific schools. The Minister noted that schools act as active platforms for inclusion rather than merely reflecting neighbourhood demographics.

results.title: Personal Explanation
results.summary: The Minister corrected a previous statement regarding three fatal fires in Singapore last year, clarifying that the Toa Payoh incident involving fire spread from the 10th to the 11th floor did not result in any deaths. This factual adjustment updates the record on fire-related fatalities for the year. No other details regarding evacuation difficulties or specific casualty figures were provided in this explanation.

results.title: Plans for Submission of National Adaptation Plan to United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change
results.summary: Singapore plans to develop and publish its inaugural National Adaptation Plan (NAP) in 2027 as a standalone document to the UNFCCC. This long-term living strategy builds on previous submissions, including the 2022 Adaptation Communication and 2025 Nationally Determined Contribution. The NAP will incorporate domestic efforts and international developments, such as the Global Goal on Adaptation framework adopted at COP30. Further details regarding the plan will be shared at the Committee of Supply in 2026.

results.title: Plans to Enhance Existing Security Measures at Places of Worship with Installation of Police Cameras
results.summary: The Police plan to deploy over 200,000 police cameras islandwide by the mid-2030s, with coverage extending to the vicinity of some places of worship as part of a broader public area strategy. Security enhancements for religious sites extend beyond camera installation through the Safety and Security Watch Group, which facilitates security audits, best practice sharing, and training with building owners and religious organisations. Under the SGSecure movement, additional support includes assistance with security self-assessments, contingency planning, and access to counter-terrorism seminars and psychological first aid training via the Crisis Preparedness for Religious Organisations programme. Public vigilance is further promoted through engagement on spotting suspicious activity and responding to emergencies at various grassroots touchpoints.

results.title: Expression of Regret at the Conduct of Mr Pritam Singh and Consideration of His Suitability to Continue as the Leader of The Opposition
results.summary: Following his High Court conviction for lying before the Committee of Privileges and guiding Ms Raeesah Khan to conceal an untruth, Parliament debated a motion to express regret at Mr Pritam Singh's conduct and consider his suitability to continue as Leader of the Opposition. Supporters argued that dishonesty before Parliament renders an MP unsuitable for office, drawing parallels to corporate governance where convicted executives are dismissed, and urged him to voluntarily step aside to uphold institutional integrity. Conversely, opponents contended that the motion constituted a political penalty beyond legal consequences, noting that the final decision on removal rests with the Prime Minister rather than Parliament. While the House affirmed that integrity is a fundamental pillar, the debate highlighted divergent views on whether Parliament should act independently of the Workers' Party's internal deliberations or if further political censure was necessary.

results.title: Possible Amendment of Prevention of Corruption Act to Prevent Liability Evasion by Senior Management of Large Corporations
results.summary: The Minister for Finance stated that the Corrupt Practices Investigation Bureau (CPIB) is reviewing the Prevention of Corruption Act following the *Public Prosecutor v China Railway Tunnel Group* case to identify potential legislative gaps. The Government will consider amending the Act to broaden corporate attribution tests or introduce a "failure to prevent" offence if necessary to prevent liability evasion by senior management of large corporations. Any decision to return to Parliament for amendments will be made only after the CPIB completes its review and determines a need for legislative change.

results.title: Possible Increase in Statutory Minimum Starting Annual Leave Entitlement to OECD Average
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower stated that the Employment Act currently mandates a statutory minimum of seven days of annual leave in the first year, increasing by one day annually up to 14 days. While the review of the Act considers raising this minimum closer to the OECD average of 20 days rather than benchmarking against Hong Kong or Taiwan, the majority of resident employees already receive more than the statutory requirement. In 2024, over 90% of full-time resident employees aged 25 to 64 were entitled to more than seven days, and 68.2% received more than 14 days. Tripartite partners will continue to review employment provisions holistically to balance worker support with business viability.

results.title: Proposal for Review of Operational Risks with Consolidation of RSAF's Assets in Fewer Airbases
results.summary: The Ministry for Defence confirmed that relocating Paya Lebar Air Base was a deliberate decision balancing national development needs with security resilience, noting that critical assets are dispersed and hardened against attacks. To mitigate risks from concentrated assets, significant resources are being invested in expanding Tengah and Changi Air Base runways and upgrading detection capabilities against drone threats. The Minister stated that operational concepts are uniquely tailored to Singapore's lack of strategic depth, ensuring timely responses despite high strike risks inherent to the island's geography. While acknowledging that drone warfare capabilities will likely become more widespread by 2030, the SAF will continually assess threats and capabilities without disclosing specific operational details for security reasons.

results.title: Proposal for Shorter COE Renewal Durations As A More Affordable Option
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport declined a proposal to introduce shorter Certificate of Entitlement (COE) renewal durations, such as annually or two-yearly, citing concerns that it would amplify COE price cycles and reduce supply for new car buyers. While acknowledging the financial strain on small businesses and hawkers facing high upfront costs, the Minister noted that commercial vehicle owners already enjoy a five-year renewal concession extendable to the vehicle's statutory life. Consequently, no new shorter-term options will be implemented for either private or commercial vehicles, as the current five-year concession is deemed sufficient to balance existing owners' interests with market access for new entrants.

results.title: Proposal to Extend Subsides for Shingles Vaccination to Those Aged 50-59 as Dementia Prevention Measure
results.summary: The Ministry of Health will not extend shingles vaccination subsidies to persons aged 50-59, maintaining the current age criterion because shingles incidence rises steeply after age 60. While observational studies suggest a potential association between vaccination and reduced dementia risk, the Ministry states additional research is required to establish causality and biological mechanisms before policy changes. Consequently, dementia prevention savings are not currently included in cost-benefit analyses for these vaccines. The Ministry will continue monitoring emerging scientific evidence regarding the vaccine's effect on dementia risk.

results.title: Recovery of Concert Ticket Scam Losses from January to October 2025 and Legal Actions Taken
results.summary: From January to October 2025, the Police seized over $190,000 from bank accounts linked to concert ticket scams, though some funds may originate from other sources. The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs clarified that the exact percentage of the $615,000 total loss recovered remains unquantified due to these mixed fund origins. Additionally, the government does not actively track the number of individuals prosecuted specifically for concert ticket scams, as prosecution data is not segmented by this specific offence type.

results.title: Recurring Complaints about Night-time Noise from Delivery Trucks in HDB Estates
results.summary: The National Environment Agency (NEA) and Housing and Development Board (HDB) address recurring night-time noise complaints from delivery trucks in HDB estates through targeted enforcement and engagement. NEA takes action under the Environmental Protection and Management (Vehicular Emissions) regulations when drivers are found idling unnecessarily, while HDB coordinates with shop owners and tenants to adjust delivery schedules where feasible. Agencies continue monitoring feedback to balance logistics needs with residents' right to undisturbed sleep, though no new enforceable guidelines covering unloading practices or equipment use were introduced during this session.

results.title: Reducing Migrant Domestic Workers' Upfront Costs for Families with Young Children or Vulnerable Elderly and Enhancing Employer Protections
results.summary: The median upfront cost to hire a new migrant domestic worker (MDW) is approximately $4,700, with half recoverable via loans, remaining stable since 2022. Families with young children or vulnerable elderly benefit from concessionary levies of about $60 monthly, saving roughly $5,760 over two years. The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) requires agencies to refund at least 50% of fees if an MDW's contract is terminated within six months without a suitable replacement. Agencies are licensed to set fees based on market competition, though fee caps apply to charges levied on MDWs. The Caregiving Grant offers $400, increasing to $600 in April 2026, while households needing further assistance can approach social services. Employers can assess MDW history via the FDW e-service portal, and serious offences like theft bar individuals from future employment.

results.title: Regional Coordination Strategy for Monitoring Emergence of New Psychoactive Substances
results.summary: Singapore has not tabled proposals to schedule etomidate or its analogues under international drug conventions because the World Health Organization Expert Committee on Drug Dependence has not yet found sufficient evidence of abuse liability or health harms to warrant listing. The Institute of Mental Health is currently collating evidence regarding etomidate's negative impacts and legitimate medical uses. Singapore maintains a temporary listing under the Misuse of Drugs Act while actively engaging with international partners, including the UN Commission on Narcotic Drugs and the UN Office on Drugs Crime's Global SMART Programme, to monitor emerging threats. Regionally, Singapore coordinates with ASEAN counterparts at ministerial and senior official levels to exchange intelligence, enforcement experiences, and best practices to ensure regional scheduling keeps pace with the proliferation of new psychoactive substances.

results.title: Regulating Owners of Large Rental Car Fleets due to Impact on Private Hire Drivers' Livelihoods After Insolvency
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority (LTA) will collaborate with the National Taxi Association, National Private Hire Vehicles Association, and National Trades Union Congress to enhance driver awareness of rental contract terms and safeguard livelihoods following the Autobahn insolvency. While the LTA declined to regulate large rental fleet owners due to potential increased compliance costs, it confirmed a workgroup will review the vocational licence curriculum to include education on contractual matters and the Platform Workers Act. Regarding deposit recovery, affected drivers remain unsecured creditors in the High Court proceedings, making full recovery unlikely; however, the tripartite group has already secured new earning opportunities through partners like GrabRentals and CDG Rent-A-Car. The Ministry of Manpower will consult on extending short-term financial assistance to this specific group.

results.title: Reintegration of Platform Workers to Traditional Employment through Support for Job Redesign Programme
results.summary: The Support for Job Redesign programme is not deployed to incentivise platform workers returning to traditional employment, as platform work arrangements differ fundamentally from Flexible Work Arrangements. Workforce Singapore does not track transitions from platform work to traditional employment through this specific programme. While the Government provides resources like the Employment and Employability Institute's career matching services and schemes such as the Career Conversion Programmes to help workers plan careers and strengthen employability, no specific targets are set for tracking annual transitions from platform work. The Ministry notes that platform workers often choose this work for greater flexibility and control, and existing schemes aim to improve overall employability rather than directly addressing pay or flexibility comparisons to incentivise transition.

results.title: Reported Complaints on Free-range Chickens or Junglefowl within Residential Estates
results.summary: Over the past five years, an average of 2,000 feedback instances regarding free-ranging chickens in residential estates were recorded. The National Parks Board (NParks) manages the population through community-based measures, habitat modification, and public education rather than relocation. The Minister confirmed that NParks has no plans to pilot relocating chickens to non-residential habitats like Jurong Island or Botanic Gardens. Consequently, existing complaints are addressed via population control and discouraging the release or feeding of free-ranging chickens.

results.title: Reports of Condensation and Mould on Walls and Ceilings due to Neighbours' Use of Air-conditioners
results.summary: In 2025, HDB received approximately 240 cases regarding condensation and mould on walls or ceilings caused by neighbours' air-conditioner use. Upon receiving feedback, HDB conducts inspections to determine causes and provides advice on air-conditioner usage, ventilation, and mould-resistant paint. While HDB will explore the efficacy of wall or ceiling enhancements, it has not mandated them; instead, it considers case-specific circumstances for compensation sharing in instances of egregious usage causing damage. The Minister indicated that HDB may issue advisories on usage duration and temperature settings but will evaluate specific cases to determine appropriate compensation mechanisms rather than establishing universal mandates or guidelines for preventive maintenance obligations.

results.title: Review of Approved Adoptions following News of Indonesian Ring Trafficking Babies to Singapore
results.summary: The Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF) is reviewing approved adoptions following Indonesian allegations of baby trafficking but cannot disclose the number of cases or provide a timeline, as investigations with Indonesian authorities are ongoing. MSF is engaging affected families to address concerns while safeguarding children's welfare, noting that adoption and citizenship processes are legally separate, with citizenship applications potentially taking up to 12 months. While adoptive parents operate under commercial agency frameworks, MSF enforces guidelines requiring agencies to conduct due diligence on child origins; agencies found knowingly importing children of suspicious origin will face necessary actions. Financial assistance for affected families is available upon request, with each case assessed individually based on circumstances.

results.title: Review of Enforcement Measures Against Pet Groomers and Proposal for Mandatory CCTV Recordings in Pet Grooming Facilities
results.summary: The Minister for National Development confirmed that the Animal and Veterinary Service (AVS) is reviewing the Animals and Birds Act (ABA) to potentially introduce mandatory CCTV recordings in pet grooming facilities and to enhance penalties for serious negligence. NParks has investigated 54 cases of alleged pet harm over the past three years, noting that existing enforcement levers include disqualification orders of up to 12 months and prosecution under sections 41 and 42 of the ABA for cruelty or duty of care failures. While mandatory certification or competency requirements were raised, the Minister stated that current levers are being reviewed alongside public feedback, with the Code of Animal Welfare serving as a benchmark for minimum standards. The AVS hotline (1800 4761 600) remains available for reporting breaches, and the review of licensing conditions to incorporate supervision and emergency protocols is ongoing.

results.title: Review of HIP Voting Framework and Alternative Upgrading Options for Ageing HDB Blocks
results.summary: The Minister for National Development confirmed that the Ministry will review the Home Improvement Programme (HIP) voting framework to address risks for blocks with fewer eligible units, potentially by grouping them into clusters to ensure sufficient participation. While the standard requirement remains 75% consent from eligible Singaporean households, HDB has already allowed re-polls for four blocks in Serangoon and Lengkong Tiga after appeals, all of which succeeded. For blocks that cannot secure HIP votes, alternative support includes the Goodwill Repair Assistance scheme for ceiling leaks and spalling concrete, as well as direct applications for senior-friendly fittings under the Enhancement for Active Seniors programme. Both schemes provide partial cost co-payment for eligible households, with a specific review of the voting process for smaller blocks currently underway.

results.title: Review of Home Equity Loan, TDSR Exemptions and Mitigating Delinquency Risks from Overseas Property Investments Speculation
results.summary: Home Equity Loans (HELs), also termed Mortgage Equity Withdrawal Loans (MEWLs), are governed by Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) residential property loan rules requiring total outstanding loans to stay within applicable loan-to-value limits. While MEWLs are generally subject to the Total Debt Servicing Ratio (TDSR), borrowers qualify for an exemption if their total debt secured by the property does not exceed 50% of the property's current market value, providing flexibility for those who have substantially paid down their mortgage. Beyond regulatory caps, MAS expects financial institutions to conduct holistic credit assessments that evaluate borrowers' repayment ability based on income, financial assets, and other obligations, alongside assessing collateral strength to mitigate delinquency risks associated with speculative overseas property investments. Consumers are encouraged to exercise financial prudence when taking on MEWLs or purchasing overseas properties.

results.title: Review of MediSave Withdrawal Limits to Pay for Private Medical Insurance in Light of Premium Increases
results.summary: MediSave usage for optional Integrated Shield Plans remains capped by Additional Withdrawal Limits (AWLs) to preserve funds for basic healthcare needs. The Minister noted that while MediShield Life premiums are fully payable via MediSave, commercial private insurance premiums are subject to these caps. Individuals facing challenges with rising premiums are advised to consult financial advisors to review coverage against their healthcare needs and financial affordability. No specific timeline for reviewing AWLs was provided, and the government will balance enhanced coverage with future savings adequacy.

results.title: Reviewing Current Animal Management Contractor Protocols and Training Standards
results.summary: The Ministry of National Development stated that the question regarding the review of force parameters and contractor training standards following a dog's death at Seletar West Farmway 8 was already addressed in a reply provided on 12 January 2026. The Minister indicated that this specific inquiry was not new and referred to the existing response given to Ms Lee Hui Ying. No new measures, timelines, or formalised collaboration plans with animal welfare groups were announced during this session.

results.title: Rethinking Redevelopment and Green Preservation
results.summary: The Government clarified that brownfield classifications do not automatically exempt projects from Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) requirements, as assessments depend on potential impacts rather than historical zoning alone. Regarding the proposed rezoning of the Serangoon River Forest area east of Sungai Serangoon for a bus depot, officials stated the site is not a sensitive nature area because it contains young, exotic-dominated secondary forest with lower ecological value, rendering an EIA unnecessary for the specific project. While the Government will not revisit the decision for this specific site, it affirmed a continued commitment to planning greenery and recreational spaces in remaining areas. The response addresses concerns about cumulative losses from densification by distinguishing between mature ecological spaces and the specific vegetation at the proposed development site.

results.title: Reviewing Fire Escape Routes for Corner HDB Units and Implementation of Regular Fire Drills
results.summary: The Minister clarified that HDB blocks comply with the Fire Code, ensuring ventilated escape routes and fire-proof doors. If routes are blocked, residents should shelter in unaffected rooms, seal door gaps, and call 995. Regular fire drills are not mandated under the Fire Safety Act. However, the Singapore Civil Defence Force and People's Association conduct block-level evacuation exercises periodically to familiarize residents with escape routes. These measures address safety protocols without imposing new mandatory drill requirements on estates.

results.title: Reviewing Income Computation Method and Considering Caregiving Expenses for Childcare Subsidy Eligibility
results.summary: ECDA assesses Additional Subsidy eligibility using gross monthly household income, which includes only the parents' earnings and excludes other family members, though larger households (five or more members) receive a more favourable per capita calculation. The Ministry confirms it actively gathers feedback from social service agencies and family service centres regarding the effectiveness of current means-testing processes for caregiving households. While no specific timeline for reviewing subsidy frameworks was provided, officials stated they will implement necessary changes based on ground-level feedback received from preschools and agencies.

results.title: Sharing of Criminal Records to Screen Prospective Employees Applying for Positions with Access to Vulnerable Groups
results.summary: The Ministry of Home Affairs shares relevant criminal records with agencies like the Ministry of Social and Family Development and the Ministry of Education. This sharing occurs specifically during pre-employment screening for positions involving access to vulnerable groups. The measure ensures that prospective employees are screened against the register of criminals before appointment. No further details on the scope of records or specific timelines were provided in the transcript.

results.title: Standardised Framework for Schools to Ensure Consistent Implementation of Expanded Digital Device Restrictions during School Hours
results.summary: The Ministry of Education addressed the query regarding enforcement mechanisms for expanded digital device restrictions effective January 2026 in its oral reply to Question No 13 on 13 January 2026. The Minister stated that specific guidelines and a standardised disciplinary framework have already been provided to schools to ensure consistent implementation across all school hours, including recess and co-curricular activities. No further details on the specific mechanisms were elaborated in this transcript.

results.title: Statistics on Beds for Homeless within Shelters and Measures to Support Transition into Long-term Residences
results.summary: Currently, seven Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF)-funded Transitional Shelters and 20 charitable Safe Sound Sleeping Places (S3Ps) provide 730 and 160 beds respectively, offering social work interventions to facilitate transitions to long-term housing such as HDB Public Rental or open market rentals. Recent data indicates an average stay of seven months for youths under 35 versus 10 months for others, with the 2025 Street Count reporting a 6% rough sleeper prevalence compared to 3% in 2022. MSF employs dedicated outreach teams via the Partners Engaging and Empowering Rough Sleepers (PEERS) Network for weekly night walks and maintains surge capacity mechanisms. While the predominant rough sleeper profile remains male, middle-aged, and often employed, the Ministry will continue monitoring trends and unpacking nuances regarding youth homelessness and couch surfing in future reports.

results.title: Statistics on HDB Rental Vacancy over Last Three Years and Potential Policy Adjustments to Reduce Vacancies
results.summary: As of 31 December 2025, approximately 2,000 (3.2%) HDB rental flats are vacant due to sprucing works, while 1,600 (2.6%) await allocation. This represents a decline from 4,800 units (7.7%) in December 2023 and 4,000 units (6.5%) in December 2024. The Ministry for National Development confirmed there are no plans to relax Public Rental Scheme eligibility criteria, as current rules ensure fair use of taxpayer-funded resources. HDB maintains a needs-based approach, reviewing requests holistically and assisting households that cannot buy a flat, lack other housing options, and have no family support.

results.title: Statistics on Public Rental Flat Applications by Singles and Outcomes of Appeals by Under-35 Applicants in Last Five Years
results.summary: Over the last five years, the Housing and Development Board (HDB) received an average of 4,000 annual public rental applications from singles, with approximately 35% successful. Applications failing eligibility criteria, such as the minimum age requirement for singles, are unsuccessful. However, HDB holistically assesses individual circumstances, including family support and social worker input. In this period, HDB assisted about 150 singles aged below 35, including widowed and orphaned applicants, to secure public rental flats based on their needs and exceptional circumstances.

results.title: Statistics on SG Culture Pass Usage and Further Plans to Encourage Redemption Rates
results.summary: As of 1 January 2026, over 1.1 million Singaporeans have registered for the SG Culture Pass, with youth (18–35 years) accounting for half of credit usage. Live events represent the largest share of utilisation at 42%, while only 33 out of 742 events recorded zero redemptions. To boost participation, MCCY will expand the programme to include SingLit books from 1 March 2026 and local film screenings from 7 November 2025. The Ministry continues partnering with community agencies like the National Youth Council to raise awareness and promote engagement with cultural offerings beyond the current scheme.

results.title: Statistics on Waitlist for Enrolment into Adult Disability Homes and Day Activity Centres, and Outcomes of Alternative Placements through SG Enable's Referral System
results.summary: As of December 2025, the Adult Disability Homes (ADHs) waitlist stands at 100 individuals with an average waiting time of nine months, while Day Activity Centres (DACs) have 370 individuals on a waitlist averaging eight months. Approximately half of the DAC waitlist are pending screening or assessments, and some remain due to declined preferred placements. SG Enable facilitates referrals to alternative facilities with available capacity or supports interim engagement through the Enabling Services Hub and Supported Transition and Engagement Programme. The Ministry of Social and Family Development plans to increase DAC capacity by 500 places by 2030 and is piloting the Enabled Living Programme to expand community living options.

results.title: Status Changes of Tax Residency among Singapore Citizens and Breakdown of Retention Rates by Age and Gender from 2014 to 2024
results.summary: The Minister stated that Singapore Citizens residing locally are assessed as tax residents, noting a lack of specific data on transitions between resident and non-resident statuses. Approximately 1.6 million citizens filed taxes in YA 2020, dropping to 1.4 million in YA 2024 due to factors like death, retirement, unemployment, or relocation. Of the 2024 filers, 55% were male and 45% female, with age-specific breakdowns not provided. The government did not track the proportion of 2014 residents remaining in 2024 by gender or age.

results.title: Statutory Fund to Financially Support Workers during Corporate Liquidations and Bankruptcies
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower stated that existing schemes provide financial support to workers affected by corporate liquidations, though these are means-tested and subject to eligibility criteria to direct resources effectively. Local workers involuntarily unemployed may access temporary assistance via the SkillsFuture Jobseeker Support scheme, while lower-income households can approach their Social Service Office. Foreign workers facing similar situations may seek financial assistance from the Migrant Workers' Centre established by tripartite partners. The Government has not indicated plans to introduce a new, non-means-tested statutory fund for this purpose.

results.title: Strategies to Balance Implementation and Benefits of Central Kitchen for Schools against Potential Food Wastage
results.summary: Under the Central Kitchen Meal Model, meals are pre-ordered and prepared centrally before delivery to schools. This demand-driven approach aligns production with actual demand to reduce over-preparation and food wastage while ensuring food safety through standardised processes. Nutritional quality is maintained via professionally developed menus adhering to the Health Promotion Board's Healthy Meals in Schools Programme guidelines. The model addresses challenges in securing canteen stallholders by improving efficiency without explicitly increasing waste.

results.title: Take-up Rate of Caregiving Respite Services
results.summary: In 2025, over 14,600 seniors and more than 3,600 persons with disabilities and children with developmental needs utilised subsidised respite care services. The Agency for Integrated Care and SG Enable prioritise urgent cases, including caregivers experiencing burnout. Later this year, Home Personal Care will expand hours to include after-hours respite options. Additionally, the Take-a-Break home-based respite programme for persons with disabilities and children with developmental needs is currently under review to better meet client needs.

results.title: Time Limit for Minister's Speech
results.summary: Members moved to exempt proceedings on a specific Motion from Standing Order 48(8), which normally imposes a time limit on Minister Indranee's speeches. This procedural request seeks to remove the standard time restriction for the Minister's address during this particular debate. The motion aims to allow extended speaking time without adhering to the usual parliamentary constraints found in Standing Order 48(8). No further details regarding the outcome or specific duration of the exemption were provided in the transcript.

results.title: Update on HDB Study on Microwave Scanning Technology for Detection and Tracing of Water Seepage
results.summary: Between 2024 and 2025, the Housing and Development Board (HDB) trialed microwave scanning technology at 68 flats to detect and trace water seepage sources in complex cases. The Ministry is currently assessing trial results to determine if the technology can be scaled up cost-effectively and how it complements existing methods. No rollout timeline or specific plans for ageing estates have been confirmed pending the assessment of trial outcomes.

results.title: Update on NTUC's Proposal and Enhanced Measures towards Worker Protections against Retrenchment Exercises
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower confirms that employee salary claims are already prioritised first under the Insolvency, Restructuring and Dissolution Act 2018, ranking above all unsecured debts except winding-up costs. For employers unable to pay due to business failure, the Short-Term Relief Fund provides financial assistance to eligible local lower-income workers, while MOM investigates and acts against employers breaching legislation or wilfully withholding salaries. Regarding NTUC's proposal for mandating pre-retrenchment notifications, MOM is currently reviewing the suggestion with tripartite partners and will provide an update in due course. The government maintains that existing measures support employees during retrenchment and company liquidation.

results.title: Update on Political Salaries Review Postponed from 2023
results.summary: The Minister for Finance deferred the review of political salaries in 2023. An update on the timing of this review was provided via a written reply to Question 2 on the Order Paper for the 12 January 2026 Parliamentary Sitting. The specific date for conducting the review remains unannounced in this transcript. No further details regarding the scope or criteria for the salary review were stated.

results.title: Singapore Sports Council (Amendment) Bill
results.summary: The Singapore Sports Council (Amendment) Bill aims to update the national sports policy framework by expanding the definition of "sport" to include physical activities, establishing safety standards, and strengthening athlete development pathways. While concerns were raised regarding potential deprioritisation of core maintenance, grassroots programmes, and affordable coaching, the Minister supported the Bill as a comprehensive, future-ready measure that better reflects current participation patterns across age groups. Specific inquiries addressed included funding continuity for senior-friendly mind sports classes, formal recognition and funding pathways for e-sports athletes, and proactive noise mitigation in facility design. SportSG clarified it will not be overly prescriptive regarding facility codes to allow flexibility for resident preferences and does not typically manufacture sports equipment for sale. The implementation depends on providing support for athletes beyond sports, active participation in programmes, and close collaboration with stakeholders to ensure a vibrant, inclusive sporting ecosystem.
